Cleartext Submission of Password

Description
A password was submitted to the application using an unencrypted HTTP connection. This makes the password vulnerable to eavesdropping by attackers on the network, potentially leading to account compromise or further attacks.

                                    cookie: showhints=1; PHPSESSID=0c7c43d5b57deba2c73877a562b74ade
                                    

                            
curl "http://192.168.10.21/mutillidae/index.php?page=user-info.php&username=alice&password=Letm3in_&user-info-php-submit-button=View%20Account%20Details" -e "http://192.168.10.21/mutillidae/index.php?page=user-info.php"
['WSTG-CONF-07']

Vulnerability found in /mutillidae/

Description HTTP Request cURL command line WSTG Code 
Password field password sent in clear text from URL http://192.168.10.21/mutillidae/?page=register.php
                                    GET /mutillidae/?page=register.php HTTP/1.1
                                    

                                    host: 192.168.10.21
                                    

                                    connection: keep-alive
                                    

                                    user-agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 6.1; rv:45.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/45.0
                                    

                                    accept-language: en-US
                                    

                                    accept-encoding: gzip, deflate, br
                                    

                                    accept: text/html,application/xhtml+xml,application/xml;q=0.9,image/webp,*/*;q=0.8
                                    

                                    cookie: showhints=2; PHPSESSID=0c7c43d5b57deba2c73877a562b74ade
                                    

                            
curl "http://192.168.10.21/mutillidae/?page=register.php"
['WSTG-CONF-07']

Vulnerability found in /mutillidae/

Description HTTP Request cURL command line WSTG Code 
Password field confirm_password sent in clear text from URL http://192.168.10.21/mutillidae/?page=register.php
                                    GET /mutillidae/?page=register.php HTTP/1.1
                                    

                                    host: 192.168.10.21
                                    

                                    connection: keep-alive
                                    

                                    user-agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 6.1; rv:45.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/45.0
                                    

                                    accept-language: en-US
                                    

                                    accept-encoding: gzip, deflate, br
                                    

                                    accept: text/html,application/xhtml+xml,application/xml;q=0.9,image/webp,*/*;q=0.8
                                    

                                    cookie: showhints=2; PHPSESSID=0c7c43d5b57deba2c73877a562b74ade
                                    

                            
curl "http://192.168.10.21/mutillidae/?page=register.php"
['WSTG-CONF-07']
Solutions
Ensure that all password submissions and sensitive data transmissions are performed exclusively over HTTPS (HTTP Secure). Implement HSTS (HTTP Strict Transport Security) to enforce the use of HTTPS and prevent downgrades to HTTP.
References
  • PortSwigger: Cleartext submission of password
  • OWASP: Transport Layer Protection
  • CWE-319: Cleartext Transmission of Sensitive Information

Content Security Policy Configuration

Description
Content Security Policy (CSP) is an added layer of security that helps to detect and mitigate certain types of attacks, including Cross Site Scripting (XSS) and data injection attacks.

Vulnerability found in /

Description HTTP Request cURL command line WSTG Code 
CSP is not set for URL: http://192.168.10.21/
                                    GET / HTTP/1.1
                                    

                                    host: 192.168.10.21
                                    

                                    connection: keep-alive
                                    

                                    user-agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 6.1; rv:45.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/45.0
                                    

                                    accept-language: en-US
                                    

                                    accept-encoding: gzip, deflate, br
                                    

                                    accept: text/html,application/xhtml+xml,application/xml;q=0.9,image/webp,*/*;q=0.8
                                    

                            
curl "http://192.168.10.21/"
['WSTG-CONF-12', 'OSHP-Content-Security-Policy']
Solutions
Configuring Content Security Policy involves adding the Content-Security-Policy HTTP header to a web page and giving it values to control what resources the user agent is allowed to load for that page.
References
  • Mozilla: Content Security Policy (CSP)
  • OWASP: Content Security Policy Cheat Sheet
  • OWASP: How to do Content Security Policy (PDF)
  • OWASP: Content Security Policy

Clickjacking Protection

Description
Clickjacking is a technique that tricks a user into clicking something different from what the user perceives, potentially revealing confidential information or taking control of their computer.

Vulnerability found in /

Description HTTP Request cURL command line WSTG Code 
X-Frame-Options is not set
                                    GET / HTTP/1.1
                                    

                                    host: 192.168.10.21
                                    

                                    connection: keep-alive
                                    

                                    user-agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 6.1; rv:45.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/45.0
                                    

                                    accept-language: en-US
                                    

                                    accept-encoding: gzip, deflate, br
                                    

                                    accept: text/html,application/xhtml+xml,application/xml;q=0.9,image/webp,*/*;q=0.8
                                    

                            
curl "http://192.168.10.21/"
['OSHP-X-Frame-Options']
Solutions
Implement X-Frame-Options or Content Security Policy (CSP) frame-ancestors directive.
References
  • OWASP: Clickjacking
  • KeyCDN: Preventing Clickjacking

MIME Type Confusion

Description
MIME type confusion can occur when a browser interprets files as a different type than intended, which could lead to security vulnerabilities like cross-site scripting (XSS).

Vulnerability found in /

Description HTTP Request cURL command line WSTG Code 
X-Content-Type-Options is not set
                                    GET / HTTP/1.1
                                    

                                    host: 192.168.10.21
                                    

                                    connection: keep-alive
                                    

                                    user-agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 6.1; rv:45.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/45.0
                                    

                                    accept-language: en-US
                                    

                                    accept-encoding: gzip, deflate, br
                                    

                                    accept: text/html,application/xhtml+xml,application/xml;q=0.9,image/webp,*/*;q=0.8
                                    

                            
curl "http://192.168.10.21/"
['OSHP-X-Content-Type-Options']
Solutions
Implement X-Content-Type-Options to prevent MIME type sniffing.
References
  • OWASP: MIME Sniffing
  • KeyCDN: Preventing MIME Type Sniffing

HttpOnly Flag cookie

Description
HttpOnly is an additional flag included in a Set-Cookie HTTP response header. Using the HttpOnly flag when generating a cookie helps mitigate the risk of client side script accessing the protected cookie (if the browser supports it).

Vulnerability found in /dvwa/

Description HTTP Request cURL command line WSTG Code 
HttpOnly flag is not set on the cookie 'PHPSESSID' set at 'http://192.168.10.21/dvwa/'
                                    GET /dvwa/ HTTP/1.1
                                    

                                    host: 192.168.10.21
                                    

                                    connection: keep-alive
                                    

                                    user-agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 6.1; rv:45.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/45.0
                                    

                                    accept-language: en-US
                                    

                                    accept-encoding: gzip, deflate, br
                                    

                                    accept: text/html,application/xhtml+xml,application/xml;q=0.9,image/webp,*/*;q=0.8
                                    

                            
curl "http://192.168.10.21/dvwa/"
['WSTG-SESS-02']

Vulnerability found in /dvwa/

Description HTTP Request cURL command line WSTG Code 
HttpOnly flag is not set on the cookie 'security' set at 'http://192.168.10.21/dvwa/'
                                    GET /dvwa/ HTTP/1.1
                                    

                                    host: 192.168.10.21
                                    

                                    connection: keep-alive
                                    

                                    user-agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 6.1; rv:45.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/45.0
                                    

                                    accept-language: en-US
                                    

                                    accept-encoding: gzip, deflate, br
                                    

                                    accept: text/html,application/xhtml+xml,application/xml;q=0.9,image/webp,*/*;q=0.8
                                    

                            
curl "http://192.168.10.21/dvwa/"
['WSTG-SESS-02']

Vulnerability found in /phpMyAdmin/index.php

Description HTTP Request cURL command line WSTG Code 
HttpOnly flag is not set on the cookie 'pma_theme' set at 'http://192.168.10.21/phpMyAdmin/index.php'
                                    POST /phpMyAdmin/index.php HTTP/1.1
                                    

                                    host: 192.168.10.21
                                    

                                    connection: keep-alive
                                    

                                    user-agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 6.1; rv:45.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/45.0
                                    

                                    accept-language: en-US
                                    

                                    accept-encoding: gzip, deflate, br
                                    

                                    accept: text/html,application/xhtml+xml,application/xml;q=0.9,image/webp,*/*;q=0.8
                                    

                                    content-type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded
                                    

                                    referer: http://192.168.10.21/phpMyAdmin/
                                    

                                    cookie: phpMyAdmin=a17ec037efa5f102393d6f2a7dad8f81d0777566; pma_lang=en-utf-8; pma_charset=utf-8; pma_theme=original; PHPSESSID=0c7c43d5b57deba2c73877a562b74ade
                                    

                                    content-length: 172
                                    

                                    Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded
                                    

                                    
                                    

                                    phpMyAdmin=a17ec037efa5f102393d6f2a7dad8f81d0777566&phpMyAdmin=a17ec037efa5f102393d6f2a7dad8f81d0777566&pma_username=alice&pma_password=Letm3in_&server=1&phpMyAdmin=a17ec037efa5f102393d6f2a7dad8f81d0777566&lang=en-utf-8&convcharset=utf-8&token=4961bbd5e7f00b118721cd27818978c5
                                    

                            
curl "http://192.168.10.21/phpMyAdmin/index.php" -e "http://192.168.10.21/phpMyAdmin/" -d "phpMyAdmin=a17ec037efa5f102393d6f2a7dad8f81d0777566&phpMyAdmin=a17ec037efa5f102393d6f2a7dad8f81d0777566&pma_username=alice&pma_password=Letm3in_&server=1&phpMyAdmin=a17ec037efa5f102393d6f2a7dad8f81d0777566&lang=en-utf-8&convcharset=utf-8&token=4961bbd5e7f00b118721cd27818978c5"
['WSTG-SESS-02']

Vulnerability found in /mutillidae/index.php

Description HTTP Request cURL command line WSTG Code 
HttpOnly flag is not set on the cookie 'showhints' set at 'http://192.168.10.21/mutillidae/index.php?do=toggle-hints&page=home.php'
                                    GET /mutillidae/index.php?do=toggle-hints&page=home.php HTTP/1.1
                                    

                                    host: 192.168.10.21
                                    

                                    connection: keep-alive
                                    

                                    user-agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 6.1; rv:45.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/45.0

curl "http://192.168.10.21/mutillidae/index.php?do=toggle-hints&page=home.php"

                                    GET /phpMyAdmin/index.php?token=4961bbd5e7f00b118721cd27818978c5 HTTP/1.1
                                    

                                    host: 192.168.10.21
                                    

                                    connection: keep-alive
                                    

                                    user-agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 6.1; rv:45.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/45.0

curl "http://192.168.10.21/phpMyAdmin/index.php?token=4961bbd5e7f00b118721cd27818978c5"
['WSTG-SESS-02']

Vulnerability found in /phpMyAdmin/index.php

Description HTTP Request cURL command line WSTG Code 
HttpOnly flag is not set on the cookie 'pmaPass-1' set at 'http://192.168.10.21/phpMyAdmin/index.php?token=4961bbd5e7f00b118721cd27818978c5'
                                    GET /phpMyAdmin/index.php?token=4961bbd5e7f00b118721cd27818978c5 HTTP/1.1
                                    

                                    host: 192.168.10.21
                                    

                                    connection: keep-alive
                                    

                                    user-agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 6.1; rv:45.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/45.0
                                    

                                    accept-language: en-US
                                    

                                    accept-encoding: gzip, deflate, br
                                    

                                    accept: text/html,application/xhtml+xml,application/xml;q=0.9,image/webp,*/*;q=0.8
                                    

                                    cookie: phpMyAdmin=a17ec037efa5f102393d6f2a7dad8f81d0777566; pma_lang=en-utf-8; pma_charset=utf-8; pma_fontsize=82%25; pmaUser-1=cOn2zi%2FL6pc%3D; pmaPass-1=y3L7dUiAxSE%3D; PHPSESSID=0c7c43d5b57deba2c73877a562b74ade
                                    

                            
curl "http://192.168.10.21/phpMyAdmin/index.php?token=4961bbd5e7f00b118721cd27818978c5"
['WSTG-SESS-02']
Solutions
While creation of the cookie, make sure to set the HttpOnly Flag to True.
References
  • OWASP: Testing for Cookies Attributes
  • OWASP: HttpOnly

Unencrypted Channels

Description
Sensitive data must be protected when it is transmitted through the network.

Vulnerability found in /

Description HTTP Request cURL command line WSTG Code 
No HTTPS redirection for this host. All HTTP requests are served in clear text.
                                    GET / HTTP/1.1
                                    

                                    host: 192.168.10.21
                                    

                                    connection: keep-alive
                                    

                                    user-agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 6.1; rv:45.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/45.0
                                    

                                    accept-language: en-US
                                    

                                    accept-encoding: gzip, deflate, br
                                    

                                    accept: text/html,application/xhtml+xml,application/xml;q=0.9,image/webp,*/*;q=0.8
                                    

                            
curl "http://192.168.10.21/"
['WSTG-CRYP-03']

Vulnerability found in /dvwa/

Description HTTP Request cURL command line WSTG Code 
Sensitive data (cookie in the response) was sent over an unencrypted HTTP connection to http://192.168.10.21/dvwa/. The server redirected, but not to an HTTPS URL, leaving the initial data and the redirection exposed.
                                    GET /dvwa/ HTTP/1.1
                                    

                                    host: 192.168.10.21
                                    

                                    connection: keep-alive
                                    

                                    user-agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 6.1; rv:45.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/45.0
                                    

                                    accept-language: en-US
                                    

                                    accept-encoding: gzip, deflate, br
                                    

                                    accept: text/html,application/xhtml+xml,application/xml;q=0.9,image/webp,*/*;q=0.8
                                    

                            
curl "http://192.168.10.21/dvwa/"
['WSTG-CRYP-03']

Vulnerability found in /phpMyAdmin/

Description HTTP Request cURL command line WSTG Code 
Sensitive data (cookie in the response) was sent over an unencrypted HTTP connection to http://192.168.10.21/phpMyAdmin/. The server did not enforce HTTPS.
                                    GET /phpMyAdmin/ HTTP/1.1
                                    

                                    host: 192.168.10.21
                                    

                                    connection: keep-alive
                                    

                                    user-agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 6.1; rv:45.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/45.0
                                    

                                    accept-language: en-US
                                    

                                    accept-encoding: gzip, deflate, br
                                    

                                    accept: text/html,application/xhtml+xml,application/xml;q=0.9,image/webp,*/*;q=0.8
                                    

                            
curl "http://192.168.10.21/phpMyAdmin/"
['WSTG-CRYP-03']

Vulnerability found in /rdiff/TWiki/TWikiHistory

Description HTTP Request cURL command line WSTG Code 
Sensitive data (GET parameters) was sent over an unencrypted HTTP connection to http://192.168.10.21/rdiff/TWiki/TWikiHistory?rev1=1.10&rev2=1.9. The server did not enforce HTTPS.
                                    GET /rdiff/TWiki/TWikiHistory?rev1=1.10&rev2=1.9 HTTP/1.1
                                    

                                    host: 192.168.10.21
                                    

                                    connection: keep-alive
                                    

                                    user-agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 6.1; rv:45.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/45.0
                                    

                                    accept-language: en-US
                                    

                                    accept-encoding: gzip, deflate, br
                                    

                                    accept: text/html,application/xhtml+xml,application/xml;q=0.9,image/webp,*/*;q=0.8
                                    

                                    cookie: PHPSESSID=0c7c43d5b57deba2c73877a562b74ade
                                    

                            
curl "http://192.168.10.21/rdiff/TWiki/TWikiHistory?rev1=1.10&rev2=1.9"
['WSTG-CRYP-03']

Vulnerability found in /mutillidae/index.php

Description HTTP Request cURL command line WSTG Code 
Sensitive data (GET parameters) was sent over an unencrypted HTTP connection to http://192.168.10.21/mutillidae/index.php?do=toggle-security&page=home.php. The server redirected, but not to an HTTPS URL, leaving the initial data and the redirection exposed.
                                    GET /mutillidae/index.php?do=toggle-security&page=home.php HTTP/1.1
                                    

                                    host: 192.168.10.21
                                    

                                    connection: keep-alive
                                    

                                    user-agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 6.1; rv:45.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/45.0
                                    

                                    accept-language: en-US
                                    

                                    accept-encoding: gzip, deflate, br
                                    

                                    accept: text/html,application/xhtml+xml,application/xml;q=0.9,image/webp,*/*;q=0.8
                                    

                                    cookie: PHPSESSID=0c7c43d5b57deba2c73877a562b74ade
                                    

                            
curl "http://192.168.10.21/mutillidae/index.php?do=toggle-security&page=home.php"
['WSTG-CRYP-03']

Vulnerability found in /dvwa/login.php

Description HTTP Request cURL command line WSTG Code 
Sensitive data (POST data) was sent over an unencrypted HTTP connection to http://192.168.10.21/dvwa/login.php. The server redirected, but not to an HTTPS URL, leaving the initial data and the redirection exposed.
                                    POST /dvwa/login.php HTTP/1.1
                                    

                                    host: 192.168.10.21
                                    

                                    connection: keep-alive
                                    

                                    user-agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 6.1; rv:45.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/45.0
                                    

                                    accept-language: en-US
                                    

                                    accept-encoding: gzip, deflate, br
                                    

                                    accept: text/html,application/xhtml+xml,application/xml;q=0.9,image/webp,*/*;q=0.8
                                    

                                    content-type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded
                                    

                                    referer: http://192.168.10.21/dvwa/login.php
                                    

                                    cookie: security=high; PHPSESSID=0c7c43d5b57deba2c73877a562b74ade
                                    

                                    content-length: 44
                                    

                                    Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded
                                    

                                    
                                    

                                    username=alice&password=Letm3in_&Login=Login
                                    

                            
curl "http://192.168.10.21/dvwa/login.php" -e "http://192.168.10.21/dvwa/login.php" -d "username=alice&password=Letm3in_&Login=Login"
['WSTG-CRYP-03']

Vulnerability found in /mutillidae/index.php

Description HTTP Request cURL command line WSTG Code 
Sensitive data (POST data) was sent over an unencrypted HTTP connection to http://192.168.10.21/mutillidae/index.php?page=text-file-viewer.php. The server did not enforce HTTPS.
                                    POST /mutillidae/index.php?page=text-file-viewer.php HTTP/1.1
                                    

                                    host: 192.168.10.21
                                    

                                    connection: keep-alive
                                    

                                    user-agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 6.1; rv:45.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/45.0
                                    

                                    accept-language: en-US
                                    

                                    accept-encoding: gzip, deflate, br
                                    

                                    accept: text/html,application/xhtml+xml,application/xml;q=0.9,image/webp,*/*;q=0.8
                                    

                                    content-type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded
                                    

                                    referer: http://192.168.10.21/mutillidae/index.php?page=text-file-viewer.php
                                    

                                    cookie: showhints=1; PHPSESSID=0c7c43d5b57deba2c73877a562b74ade
                                    

                                    content-length: 111
                                    

                                    Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded
                                    

                                    
                                    

                                    text-file-viewer-php-submit-button=View%20File&textfile=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.textfiles.com%2Fhacking%2Fhacking101.hac
                                    

                            
curl "http://192.168.10.21/mutillidae/index.php?page=text-file-viewer.php" -e "http://192.168.10.21/mutillidae/index.php?page=text-file-viewer.php" -d "text-file-viewer-php-submit-button=View%20File&textfile=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.textfiles.com%2Fhacking%2Fhacking101.hac"
['WSTG-CRYP-03']
Solutions
Use HTTPS for the whole web site and redirect any HTTP requests to HTTPS.
References
  • Testing for Sensitive Information Sent via Unencrypted Channels
  • Testing for Weak Transport Layer Security

Information Disclosure - Full Path

Description
The application response discloses full system paths. This information can help attackers understand the server environment, directory structure, and operating system, which can facilitate further attacks.

Vulnerability found in /twiki/TWikiHistory.html

Description HTTP Request cURL command line WSTG Code 
Response contains potential system path: /bin/view/Web.TopicName
                                    GET /twiki/TWikiHistory.html HTTP/1.1
                                    

                                    host: 192.168.10.21
                                    

                                    connection: keep-alive
                                    

                                    user-agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 6.1; rv:45.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/45.0
                                    

                                    accept-language: en-US
                                    

                                    accept-encoding: gzip, deflate, br
                                    

                                    accept: text/html,application/xhtml+xml,application/xml;q=0.9,image/webp,*/*;q=0.8
                                    

                            
curl "http://192.168.10.21/twiki/TWikiHistory.html"
['WSTG-ERRH-01']

Vulnerability found in /twiki/TWikiHistory.html

Description HTTP Request cURL command line WSTG Code 
Response contains potential system path: /bin/view/Web/TopicName
                                    GET /twiki/TWikiHistory.html HTTP/1.1
                                    

                                    host: 192.168.10.21
                                    

                                    connection: keep-alive
                                    

                                    user-agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 6.1; rv:45.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/45.0
                                    

                                    accept-language: en-US
                                    

                                    accept-encoding: gzip, deflate, br
                                    

                                    accept: text/html,application/xhtml+xml,application/xml;q=0.9,image/webp,*/*;q=0.8
                                    

                            
curl "http://192.168.10.21/twiki/TWikiHistory.html"
['WSTG-ERRH-01']

Response contains potential system path: /home/httpd/twiki

                                    accept: text/html,application/xhtml+xml,application/xml;q=0.9,image/webp,*/*;q=0.8

Vulnerability found in /twiki/TWikiDocumentation.html

Description HTTP Request cURL command line WSTG Code 
Response contains potential system path: /home/httpd/twiki/bin
                                    GET /twiki/TWikiDocumentation.html HTTP/1.1
                                    

                                    host: 192.168.10.21

                                    connection: keep-alive
                                    

                                    user-agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 6.1; rv:45.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/45.0

                                    accept-encoding: gzip, deflate, br
                                    

                                    accept: text/html,application/xhtml+xml,application/xml;q=0.9,image/webp,*/*;q=0.8
                                    

                                    cookie: showhints=1; PHPSESSID=0c7c43d5b57deba2c73877a562b74ade
                                    

                            
curl "http://192.168.10.21/mutillidae/index.php?page=user-info.php&username=alice&password=Letm3in_&user-info-php-submit-button=View%20Account%20Details" -e "http://192.168.10.21/mutillidae/index.php?page=user-info.php"

Vulnerability found in /mutillidae/index.php

Description HTTP Request cURL command line WSTG Code 
Response contains potential system path: /var/www/mutillidae/process-login-attempt.php
                                    POST /mutillidae/index.php?page=login.php HTTP/1.1
                                    

                                    host: 192.168.10.21
                                    

                                    connection: keep-alive
                                    

                                    user-agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 6.1; rv:45.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/45.0
                                    

                                    accept-language: en-US
                                    

                                    accept-encoding: gzip, deflate, br
                                    

                                    accept: text/html,application/xhtml+xml,application/xml;q=0.9,image/webp,*/*;q=0.8
                                    

                                    content-type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded
                                    

                                    referer: http://192.168.10.21/mutillidae/index.php?page=login.php
                                    

                                    cookie: showhints=0; PHPSESSID=0c7c43d5b57deba2c73877a562b74ade
                                    

                                    content-length: 62
                                    

                                    Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded
                                    

                                    
                                    

                                    username=alice&password=Letm3in_&login-php-submit-button=Login
                                    

                            
curl "http://192.168.10.21/mutillidae/index.php?page=login.php" -e "http://192.168.10.21/mutillidae/index.php?page=login.php" -d "username=alice&password=Letm3in_&login-php-submit-button=Login"
['WSTG-ERRH-01']
Solutions
Ensure that error messages and application responses do not disclose full filesystem paths or other sensitive system information. Use generic error messages for end users and log detailed errors only on the server side.
References
  • OWASP: Information Leakage
  • CWE-209: Generation of Error Message Containing Sensitive Information
  • WASC-13: Information Leakage

Secure Flag cookie

Description
The secure flag is an option that can be set by the application server when sending a new cookie to the user within an HTTP Response. The purpose of the secure flag is to prevent cookies from being observed by unauthorized parties due to the transmission of a the cookie in clear text.

Vulnerability found in /dvwa/

Description HTTP Request cURL command line WSTG Code 
Secure flag is not set on the cookie: 'PHPSESSID' set at 'http://192.168.10.21/dvwa/'
                                    GET /dvwa/ HTTP/1.1
                                    

                                    host: 192.168.10.21
                                    

                                    connection: keep-alive
                                    

                                    user-agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 6.1; rv:45.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/45.0
                                    

                                    accept-language: en-US
                                    

                                    accept-encoding: gzip, deflate, br
                                    

                                    accept: text/html,application/xhtml+xml,application/xml;q=0.9,image/webp,*/*;q=0.8
                                    

                            
curl "http://192.168.10.21/dvwa/"
['WSTG-SESS-02']

Vulnerability found in /mutillidae/index.php

Description HTTP Request cURL command line WSTG Code 
Secure flag is not set on the cookie: 'showhints' set at 'http://192.168.10.21/mutillidae/index.php?do=toggle-hints&page=home.php'
                                    GET /mutillidae/index.php?do=toggle-hints&page=home.php HTTP/1.1
                                    

                                    host: 192.168.10.21
                                    

                                    connection: keep-alive
                                    

                                    user-agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 6.1; rv:45.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/45.0
                                    

                                    accept-language: en-US
                                    

                                    accept-encoding: gzip, deflate, br
                                    

                                    accept: text/html,application/xhtml+xml,application/xml;q=0.9,image/webp,*/*;q=0.8
                                    

                                    cookie: PHPSESSID=0c7c43d5b57deba2c73877a562b74ade
                                    

                            
curl "http://192.168.10.21/mutillidae/index.php?do=toggle-hints&page=home.php"
['WSTG-SESS-02']
Solutions
When generating the cookie, make sure to set the Secure Flag to True.
References
  • OWASP: Testing for Cookies Attributes
  • OWASP: Secure Cookie Attribute

Unrestricted File Upload

Description
File upload vulnerabilities are when a web server allows users to upload files to its filesystem without sufficiently validating things like their name, type, contents, or size. Failing to properly enforce restrictions on these could allow an attacker to upload potentially dangerous files. This could even include server-side script files that enable remote code execution.

Vulnerability found in /twiki/bin/upload/Main/WebHome

Description HTTP Request cURL command line WSTG Code 
Unrestricted file upload vulnerability in the parameter filepath
                                    POST /twiki/bin/upload/Main/WebHome HTTP/1.1
                                    

                                    host: 192.168.10.21
                                    

                                    connection: keep-alive
                                    

                                    user-agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 6.1; rv:45.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/45.0
                                    

                                    accept-language: en-US
                                    

                                    accept-encoding: gzip, deflate, br
                                    

                                    accept: text/html,application/xhtml+xml,application/xml;q=0.9,image/webp,*/*;q=0.8
                                    

                                    referer: http://192.168.10.21/twiki/bin/attach/Main/WebHome
                                    

                                    cookie: PHPSESSID=0c7c43d5b57deba2c73877a562b74ade
                                    

                                    content-length: 735
                                    

                                    content-type: multipart/form-data; boundary=4460f6d987a94f0760ccd9ac17f03bd7
                                    

                                    Content-Type: multipart/form-data; boundary=------------------------boundarystring
                                    

                                    
                                    

                                    ------------------------boundarystring
                                    

                                    Content-Disposition: form-data; name="filename"
                                    

                                    
                                    

                                    default
                                    

                                    ------------------------boundarystring
                                    

                                    Content-Disposition: form-data; name="filecomment"
                                    

                                    
                                    

                                    default
                                    

                                    ------------------------boundarystring
                                    

                                    Content-Disposition: form-data; name="createlink"
                                    

                                    
                                    

                                    default
                                    

                                    ------------------------boundarystring
                                    

                                    Content-Disposition: form-data; name="hidefile"
                                    

                                    
                                    

                                    default
                                    

                                    ------------------------boundarystring
                                    

                                    Content-Disposition: form-data; name="filepath"; filename="wg6mw0hbdr.php"
                                    

                                    
                                    

                                    �PNG
                                    

                                    
                                    

                                    ␀␀␀
IHDR␀<?php $a = array(0 => "8c6r",1 => "w97",2 => "pvgzr",3 => "gbjh",4 => "h7m",5 => "sjdjg");$b = array(1,2,4,3,0,5); foreach ($b as $v) echo $a[$v]; ?>
                                    

                                    ------------------------boundarystring--
                                    

                            
curl "http://192.168.10.21/twiki/bin/upload/Main/WebHome" -e "http://192.168.10.21/twiki/bin/attach/Main/WebHome" -F "filename=default" -F "filecomment=default" -F "createlink=default" -F "hidefile=default" -F "filepath=@your_local_file;filename=wg6mw0hbdr.php"
['WSTG-BUSL-08']
Solutions
Check the file extension against a whitelist of permitted extensions rather than a blacklist of prohibited ones. Make sure the filename doesn't contain any substrings that may be interpreted as a directory or a traversal sequence (../). Rename uploaded files to avoid collisions that may cause existing files to be overwritten. Do not upload files to the server's permanent filesystem until they have been fully validated.
References
  • HackTricks: File Upload
  • PortSwigger : File upload vulnerabilities
  • CWE-434: Unrestricted Upload of File with Dangerous Type
  • OWASP: Test Upload of Unexpected File Types
